title: centaurea scabiosa subsp. alpestris
intro: Centaurea scabiosa subsp. alpestris, also known as alpine knapweed or mountain scabiosa, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, native to the mountainous regions of Europe. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to its high content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The medicinal properties of Centaurea scabiosa subsp. alpestris may provide various health benefits, including the reduction of inflammation, improvement of digestion, and support for the immune system. The plant can be used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
